scenario: Cross-project intake uses previous-scan comparison to expose stale observed files and preferred commands
contextMode: fresh temporary scan with saved report passed as --previous-scan
fixtureState: Sibling Android project observed read-only; generated report output and local paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Check whether a saved sibling-project Habitat report still matches current command guidance before selecting local Habitat work.
relevantHabitatSignals: The saved Android report was stale after project guidance changed.
--previous-scan surfaced observed-file freshness drift so the previous report could be treated as stale context.
A fresh temporary scan selected the project-local validation wrapper as the only preferred command.
--previous-scan surfaced that the old preferred command set contained raw Gradle peers that the fresh scan removed.
The fresh comparison changed the agent's next command from raw package-manager fallback toward the wrapper entrypoint and policy review.
No watched-project implementation, docs, ledger, or habitat-report output was edited.

prefer: use --previous-scan when a saved report exists and may be stale
trust current preferred commands over stale saved report guidance
use the project-local validation wrapper before raw Gradle peers
askFirst: Git/GitHub mutation before publishing Habitat-side evidence
dependency or environment mutation in watched projects
doNot: copy raw watched-project report output into Habitat or Nenrin
run stale raw Gradle peers just because an older saved report listed them
reviewFirst: command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing the Habitat-side fixture

withSavedReportOnly.firstProposedAction: Treat the saved report as bounded-stale because a key guidance file changed after the saved scan.
commandsProposed: compare scanned-at time with key file mtimes
withSavedReportOnly.risk: Trusting the saved report could keep raw Gradle peers in the agent's first validation choice after current guidance narrowed the wrapper entrypoint.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Run a fresh scan into temporary output with the saved report as --previous-scan, then follow only the current preferred command set.
commandsProposed: record previous-scan preferred-command drift as Habitat-side behavior evidence
withHabitatContext.previousScanFreshnessChangeSummary: Observed project files changed: modified docs/current_status.md, docs/development_automation.md, docs/development_loop.md.
withHabitatContext.previousScanFreshnessChangeImpact: Treat the previous report as stale context; use the current generated context before choosing commands.
withHabitatContext.previousScanChangeSummary: Preferred commands changed from ./scripts/assemble-debug.sh, ./gradlew test, ./gradlew build to ./scripts/assemble-debug.sh.
withHabitatContext.previousScanChangeImpact: Re-check command_policy.md; use only current allowed preferred commands.
avoidedCommands: copy raw report output into Nenrin
run raw ./gradlew before checking the documented validation wrapper

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No new scanner breadth is justified. The useful behavior is consumption-side: when a saved cross-project report exists, --previous-scan can turn stale observed files and command drift into short, AI-actionable notes without editing the watched project.

verdict.reason: The fresh comparison exposed stale observed-file context plus stale preferred-command peers, kept the current wrapper as the only preferred command, and avoided watched-project mutation or broad Android expansion.
verdict.followUpImprovement: If agents still hand-compare stale reports instead of using --previous-scan, update automation wording before adding report lifecycle features.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If previous-scan preferred-command drift is not visible enough in agent_context.md, tighten the previous-scan note before adding broader stale-report automation.
